description_long: This is a rectangular, portable radio receiver with a black plastic casing and a prominent telescopic metal antenna extending from the top right corner. The front face features a large speaker grille occupying the lower half, while the upper half displays a frequency indicator panel with blue and white markings for AM/FM bands. Two rotary knobs, likely for tuning and volume control, are situated on the lower right of the front panel. The back is plain with some molded text and a battery compartment cover, while the sides are flat and unadorned. The overall design is utilitarian, typical of mid-to-late 20th-century portable radios, and is suitable for use in urban environments such as parks, garages, or street corners.
description: A black, rectangular portable radio with a telescopic antenna, front speaker grille, frequency display, and two control knobs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the speaker grille, frequency indicator, two control knobs, and the extended antenna.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the slim profile and the protruding antenna.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a plain back with molded text and the antenna base.
description_view_3: Right side view highlighting the radio's depth and the side of the control knobs.
